title: Address AgentOps review findings
description: Fix review findings in the AgentOps JSON runner so ID-based writes return real calendar metadata and timestamp responses preserve RFC3339Nano precision.
close_reason: Addressed AgentOps review findings for calendar_id metadata and RFC3339Nano timestamp responses.

title: Add OpenPlanner AgentOps eval harness and first production report
description: Add an isolated OpenPlanner agent eval harness for the production AgentOps JSON runner surface, with parallel execution, reduced JSON/Markdown reports, and a first production smoke/full report path that uses repo-relative artifacts and run-root placeholders.
acceptance_criteria: Harness runs production AgentOps scenarios; --parallel defaults to 4; result order is deterministic; reports include parallelism and elapsed metadata; first production report uses repo-relative paths and <run-root> placeholders.
close_reason: Implemented AgentOps JSON runner pivot, production skill/docs cleanup, eval harness with smoke report, and passing quality gates.

title: Pivot OpenPlanner agent surface to AgentOps JSON runner
description: Pivot the production agent-facing OpenPlanner surface to an AgentOps package plus machine-facing JSON runner. The runner must cover routine planning actions, the production skill must use only that runner, stale generated/CLI-oriented docs must be removed, an eval harness/report surface must exist, and quality gates must pass.
acceptance_criteria: AgentOps package and runner exist; production skill uses only the runner; stale generated/CLI docs are removed; eval harness/report exists; quality gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented AgentOps JSON runner pivot, production skill/docs cleanup, eval harness with smoke report, and passing quality gates.

title: Add code-first OpenPlanner SDK helpers
description: Implement the code-first SDK helper facade, docs, skill guidance, agent eval documentation, and tests mirroring the latest OpenHealth SDK helper direction.
close_reason: Implemented code-first OpenPlanner SDK helpers, docs, skill guidance, agent eval documentation, and tests.

title: Require local commit before AGENTS.md manual review pause
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:

The session completion workflow in AGENTS.md currently pauses for manual review before a local git commit exists. Update the workflow so agents must stage intended files and create a local commit before the manual review pause, then renumber downstream steps and references so the pause point is step 5 instead of step 4.

Acceptance criteria:
- Session Completion intro says complete steps 1-5 before stopping and pause at step 5
- A new local-only commit step is inserted after update issue status
- Downstream steps are renumbered sequentially through 9
- Critical-rules wording matches manual review after step 5 and does not imply pushing before review

title: Tighten SDK-first install story and first release docs
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

OpenPlanner already behaves as an in-process Go SDK, but the install story, package docs, smoke-test wording, and first-tag release guidance still need to be made fully consistent before the first public release. Update the public docs around the SDK-first flow, add package-level sdk docs, align OpenClaw/release-verification wording, and keep the temp-module smoke test aligned with the documented consumer flow without changing runtime behavior.
notes: Implemented SDK-first install-story updates in README, docs/maintainers.md, docs/release-verification.md, interfaces/openclaw/SKILL.md, sdk/doc.go, and sdk/packaging_smoke_test.go. Verification: make check passed. Local commit: 8131f6a (Tighten the SDK-first install story). Paused for manual review before any push, workflow_dispatch release run, or v0.1.0 tag creation. New direction: first-release docs must be revised around the AgentOps JSON runner as the production agent surface while keeping the SDK as the Go developer surface.

title: Address review findings in pagination and recurrence validation
description: Review feedback identified four discrete bugs in the new spec-first API surface: invalid or stale cursors restart pagination, timed untilDate recurrences allow one extra occurrence at next midnight, malformed or out-of-range limit values are silently normalized instead of rejected, and list endpoints accept invalid calendarId filters. Fix the service, recurrence, and router layers and add regression coverage.

title: Implement spec-first generated Go client foundation
description: Build the first shippable OpenPlanner SDK around an OpenAPI source of truth, generated Go client, embedded local transport, SQLite-backed calendar/event/task storage, recurrence support, agenda queries, docs, and CI checks.

title: Harden releases when the first distributable artifact exists
description: Once the project ships a distributable artifact, strengthen release authenticity and supply-chain evidence. This includes signed tags or artifacts, provenance, SBOM publication, checksums, and making the build reproducible enough to verify release integrity.
acceptance_criteria: Release process includes signing or attestations appropriate to the artifact type, publishes an SBOM and checksums when relevant, and documents how users can verify release integrity.
close_reason: Release automation now verifies the repo, packages deterministic source assets, publishes checksums and an SBOM, and adds GitHub attestations for the source release.

title: Add deployment and publishing trust boundaries when deploys exist
description: If the project introduces deployment targets or package publishing, establish separate trust boundaries for build, test, and release. Move to short-lived OIDC credentials, protected environments, and tight workflow permissions for tags, releases, packages, and deployments.
acceptance_criteria: Release and deploy workflows use protected environments where applicable, avoid long-lived secrets, and scope write permissions only to the workflows that need them.
close_reason: The release workflow is now split into verify, package/attest, and publish stages, with write permissions and the release environment scoped only to the publish edge and OIDC-based attestations used instead of long-lived secrets.

title: Add real contributor gates when the first runnable package or app lands
description: When the repository gains its first runnable package or application, add truthful contributor gates tied to that surface. This includes real test commands, local run instructions, compatibility expectations, support policy details, and language-appropriate code scanning or linting.
acceptance_criteria: Contributor docs include real setup and validation commands; CI enforces the actual checks needed for the first runnable component; support and compatibility expectations match the shipped surface.
close_reason: Contributor docs and CI now reflect the real shipped surface: in-process Go SDK usage, runnable install/verification commands, packaging smoke coverage, and govulncheck as part of make check.

title: Open-source readiness backlog
description: Track readiness items that should not be implemented until the repository grows beyond the current scaffold stage. This issue groups deferred work around real runtime checks, stronger release integrity, deployment trust boundaries, maintainer isolation, and ongoing security operations.

title: Initialize Go project and refresh toolchain management
description: Why: the repository is currently empty and needs a public-safe scaffold plus a basic Go module and maintained tooling so maintainers can bootstrap a consistent local and CI environment. What: add the initial public repository scaffold, initialize a Go module, add a minimal runnable entrypoint, pin local tooling, seed the shared Beads backlog, and wire the repository to GitHub.
notes: Local scaffold is complete. The public GitHub repo exists, origin points at git@github.com:yazanabuashour/openplanner.git, Beads is initialized in embedded mode with prefix/database op, the six deferred readiness issues are seeded with matching suffixes and dependencies, and the baseline Go module, CLI, docs, and GitHub workflow files are in place. Local verification completed with mise install, make check, go run ./cmd/openplanner, bd list, bd show op-2rz, and git remote -v. Paused before git pull --rebase, bd dolt push, and git push for manual review per AGENTS workflow.

title: Honor explicit query database path before default resolution
description: Fix examples/openplanner/query so an explicit -db path can be used even in environments where sdk.DefaultDatabasePath cannot resolve HOME or XDG_DATA_HOME. Keep the helper example behavior unchanged when -db is omitted.
close_reason: Resolved by parsing flags before resolving the default database path.

title: Improve OpenPlanner skill common-query usability
description: Apply observed Agent Skill usability feedback to the OpenPlanner skill. Add common-query guidance for default database discovery, calendar/event/task listing, agenda range queries, and task occurrence completion. Add a narrow runnable example under examples/openplanner/query that uses the existing SDK without changing public SDK or OpenAPI surfaces.
close_reason: Implemented common-query skill docs and the read-only query example.

title: Pin and automate Agent Skills validation for openplanner
description: The repository now ships a spec-compliant openplanner skill under skills/openplanner, but validation is still manual because the Agent Skills validator is not part of the pinned toolchain or CI. Track follow-up work to choose a validator, pin it in the local toolchain, and add a repeatable repo check without weakening the current release workflow.
notes: Agent Skills validation should assert the OpenPlanner production skill documents cmd/openplanner-agentops, keeps routine tasks on the AgentOps JSON runner, and omits stale generated-client, temp-module, CLI, direct-SQLite, and module-cache instructions.

title: Package OpenPlanner for native OpenClaw installer metadata
description: OpenPlanner currently ships a Go module plus OpenClaw-facing docs, but it does not yet publish installer metadata or a dedicated binary/plugin surface for OpenClaw. Track the packaging work once the install target is ready so agents can discover and install OpenPlanner without manual module setup.
notes: OpenClaw's docs confirm a valid skill is just an AgentSkills-compatible folder with a SKILL.md and YAML frontmatter, loaded from a supported skills directory. Native installer metadata is optional and only needed for the macOS Skills UI / gateway-backed install path via metadata.openclaw.install. For openplanner, the remaining work is deciding whether to ship a real OpenClaw skill wrapper in a supported skills directory; if so, installer metadata is optional rather than required. New dependency: package/discovery metadata should wait for the AgentOps JSON runner pivot so installers expose the final production skill shape.

title: Add explicit patch clear semantics for optional planning fields
description: The first OpenPlanner spec supports patching optional strings by sending empty strings, but recurrence and other optional objects cannot be explicitly cleared because the current OpenAPI shape only distinguishes present vs omitted values. Add contract and handler support for explicit null/clear semantics without breaking generated clients.

title: Raise maintainer isolation as automation grows
description: As repository automation expands, reduce maintainer and bot blast radius. This includes dedicated low-privilege bot identities, stronger first-time contributor approval rules if warranted, self-hosted runner policy, and stricter review enforcement once more than one maintainer can satisfy it.
acceptance_criteria: Maintainer and automation identities follow least privilege, untrusted PR execution policy is documented, and branch/review enforcement matches the actual maintainer model without making the repo unworkable.

title: Establish ongoing security operations
description: Move beyond one-time security setup once the project has meaningful runtime or release surfaces. This includes a recurring dependency review cadence, advisory workflow rehearsal, and threat modeling, abuse-case testing, or fuzzing for sensitive functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Maintainers have a documented recurring security review cadence and, where relevant, a deeper testing plan for high-risk project surfaces.
